content:
I have been following Wedson's mentorship session on Youtube. I edited Kconfig and Makefile in ~/linux/samples/rust as directed. However when I run make menuconfig in ~/linux, I cannot access the rust samples. Searching "scull" yields a path as expected.
Screenshot-2023-11-28-at-08.22.52.png
However, upon including and selecting Sample kernel code, there is no option for Rust modules.
Screenshot-2023-11-28-at-08.24.04.png
How can I fix this?
What does make LLVM=1 rustavailable print for you?

Alice Ryhl said:

What does make LLVM=1 rustavailable print for you?

It says Rust is available!
Screenshot-2023-11-28-at-08.43.43.png
Turns out my .config file was improperly configured. Running allnoconfig qemu-busybox-min.config rust.config again resolved the issue. Thank you for your time and prompt response @Alice Ryhl.

Above you said you used make menuconfig -- please note that you need LLVM=1 in all commands, including menuconfig and others, i.e. always write it (if you are using LLVM, of course, which you probably should, since it is the recommended setup).
Miguel Ojeda has marked this topic as resolved.
